news.headline: uniQure (QURE) Now Trades Above Golden Cross: Time to Buy?
news.summary: QURE shares formed a bullish "golden cross" technical pattern after gaining roughly 72% over the prior four weeks on the back of the FDA regulatory reversal. Zacks noted improving earnings-estimate revisions alongside the technical breakout.

news.headline: uniQure Announces Closing of Upsized Public Offering
news.summary: uniQure closed an upsized underwritten offering of 5,686,813 ordinary shares/pre-funded warrants at $45.50 each, raising roughly $259 million in gross proceeds to fund the AMT-130 BLA filing and commercial preparations.

news.headline: uniQure stock rating reiterated at Buy by H.C. Wainwright on FDA update; price target raised to $80
news.summary: H.C. Wainwright raised its price target to $80 from $50 (Mizuho separately raised its target to $68 from $35), arguing the June 17 FDA update re-establishes AMT-130 as the leading disease-modifying Huntington's candidate with a clear near-term U.S. filing path.

news.headline: uniQure Commences $150 Million Proposed Public Offering
news.summary: uniQure launched an underwritten public offering targeting $150 million to bolster its cash position following the positive AMT-130 regulatory news, which was subsequently upsized to roughly $259 million gross.

news.headline: uniQure Announces Preliminary Data on the First Cohort in the Phase I/IIa Clinical Trial of AMT-260 in Refractory Mesial Temporal Lobe Epilepsy
news.summary: In the low-dose cohort of six patients, three achieved 79%-100% reductions in disabling seizures at months four to six of follow-up, with no serious treatment-related adverse events reported. Enrollment in a higher-dose cohort is ongoing, with updated data expected in H1 2027.

news.headline: uniQure Announces Plan for BLA Submission for AMT-130 in Huntington's Disease
news.summary: The FDA told uniQure that the ~3-year Phase I/II AMT-130 data (showing a 75% slowing of disease progression at the high dose vs. external control) can serve as the primary basis for a BLA seeking accelerated approval, with submission planned for Q3 2026; shares surged roughly 78% on the news.

catalysts.label: U.S. BLA submission for AMT-130
catalysts.detail: uniQure plans to submit a Biologics License Application seeking accelerated approval of AMT-130 in early manifest Huntington's disease, based on ~3-year Phase I/II data, pending final alignment with the FDA on confirmatory study design.

catalysts.label: UK MHRA Marketing Authorization Application (MAA) filing
catalysts.detail: Following a successful pre-submission meeting, uniQure plans to file an MAA with the UK's MHRA for AMT-130 in Huntington's disease, offering a parallel regulatory pathway outside the U.S.

catalysts.label: 4-year AMT-130 data analysis
catalysts.detail: uniQure has guided to a statistical analysis of 4-year Phase I/II follow-up data for AMT-130, to be discussed with the FDA and potentially support the BLA/confirmatory study design.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings report
catalysts.detail: uniQure is expected to report second-quarter 2026 financial results and provide further updates on BLA/MAA filing progress and cash runway (estimated date per consensus calendars, not yet formally confirmed by the company).

catalysts.label: Updated AMT-260 epilepsy data (higher-dose cohort)
catalysts.detail: uniQure expects to report updated Phase I/IIa data from the higher-dose (3x10^12 gc/mL) cohort of the AMT-260 refractory mesial temporal lobe epilepsy trial.

catalysts.label: Potential FDA decision on AMT-130 BLA
catalysts.detail: Assuming a Q3 2026 filing and standard/rolling review (potentially faster with priority review), an FDA action date could fall around mid-2027, though no PDUFA date has been set and timing depends on confirmatory-study alignment.

catalysts.label: Additional international regulatory pathway updates
catalysts.detail: uniQure has said it is pursuing further regulatory pathways for AMT-130 outside the U.S./UK and expects to provide updates in the second half of 2026.

pulse: uniQure has been transformed since mid-June 2026, when the FDA reversed course and agreed that ~3-year Phase I/II data can support an accelerated-approval BLA for AMT-130 in Huntington's disease, sending shares up roughly 180% over 90 days and prompting a ~$259M equity raise plus a wave of analyst price-target hikes (H.C. Wainwright to $80, Mizuho to $68). The company is now racing toward parallel Q3 2026 U.S. (BLA) and UK (MAA) filings while also advancing early-stage AMT-260 gene therapy data in epilepsy, though final FDA alignment on the confirmatory study design and the eventual approval timeline remain unresolved risks. Sentiment and momentum are strongly positive, but the stock's re-rating already prices in a substantial probability of successful accelerated approval.
